title: Customers With Above-Average Latest Order
description: Find customers whose most recent order amount is greater than the average order amount across all orders. Assume each customer has at most one order on a given date. Return the customer name along with the amount of their latest order.
sql_solution: WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, amount, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY customer_id ORDER BY date DESC) rn FROM orders), avg_val AS (SELECT AVG(amount) avg_amt FROM orders) SELECT c.name, l.amount FROM latest l JOIN customers c ON l.customer_id = c.id CROSS JOIN avg_val a WHERE l.rn = 1 AND l.amount > a.avg_amt;
pyspark_solution:
from pyspark.sql.functions import *
from pyspark.sql.window import Window

latest_window = Window.partitionBy("customer_id").orderBy(to_date(col("date")).desc())

latest_orders = orders.withColumn("rn", row_number().over(latest_window))
average_order_amount = orders.agg(avg("amount").alias("avg_amt"))

result = (
    latest_orders.alias("l")
    .join(customers.alias("c"), col("l.customer_id") == col("c.id"), "inner")
    .crossJoin(average_order_amount)
    .filter((col("l.rn") == 1) & (col("l.amount") > col("avg_amt")))
    .select(col("c.name").alias("name"), col("l.amount").alias("amount"))
)
